task_completion.description: Agent creates a Python script to simulate the PV system using PySAM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing a Python script with PySAM imports (e.g., `pysam.Pvwattsv8` or similar) and PV system parameters like 100 MW DC (or 100000 kW), 1.3 DC:AC ratio, and 14% losses.

task_completion.description: Agent implements battery dispatch and TOU pricing logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing the TOU rates ($0.04, $0.08, $0.18, $0.30), grid export limit (50 MW), and battery parameters (50 MW power rating, 85% efficiency, 10% min SOC).

task_completion.description: Agent implements the 15-year NPV calculation for the battery scenarios.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing a loop over battery capacities `[0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 300]`, battery cost of $200/kWh (or $200,000/MWh), 8% discount rate, and a 15-year analysis period.

task_completion.description: Agent runs the Python script successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of the Python script (e.g., `python script.py`) without errors, possibly with print statements showing progress or intermediate results.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the final results to the specified JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal, file explorer, or editor showing the creation or contents of `/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/curtailment_battery_optimization.json`, containing keys like `pv_generation_before_curtailment_mwh`, `battery_scenarios`, and `optimal_capacity_mwh`.

integrity.description: The agent must generate the JSON output programmatically, not by manually typing the expected values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python script contains the logic to compute the values and write to JSON using `json.dump` or similar, rather than a hardcoded string of the final output.

integrity.description: The agent must actually use PySAM to generate the hourly data, not dummy data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script calls PySAM execution methods (e.g., `system.execute()`) and processes the hourly AC power output array.

integrity.description: The script must be executed in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the command to run the script and any output, rather than just an open text editor with no proof of execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The optimal battery capacity is 150 MWh. The no-battery PV generation is approximately 221,261 MWh, with no-battery curtailment around 44,217 MWh and no-battery revenue around $16.1M. The NPV for the optimal 150 MWh capacity is approximately $29.9M. The agent must test capacities [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 300] MWh.
